sections.paragraph:
a root not used in Kal. Syriac ܒܥܶܬ to fear, to be afraid, to dread.

Piel בִּעַת fut. יְבַעֵת-

(1) to frighten, to terrify, only poet. Psa 18:5 Job 3:5 6:4 [subst.] 7:14 9:34 13:11 , 21 Job 13:21 15:24 Isa 21:4.

(2) suddenly to come upon any one. 1Sa 16:14, בִּעֲתַתּוּ רוּחַ רָעָה מֵאֵת יְהֹוָה “there suddenly came upon him an evil spirit sent from Jehovah;” verse 15 1Sa 16:15. (Arab. بَغَتَ to come suddenly, to happen unexpectedly, with an acc. III. to attack unexpectedly; بَغْتَةً suddenly.)

Niphal, to be frightened, terrified, Dan 8:17, followed by מִפְּנֵי 1Ch 21:30 Est 7:6. [Hence]-
